relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Job’s spirit feels broken and his days extinguished.
Mockers surround him and provoke his eyes.
He asks God himself to become his pledge and guarantor.
The friends’ hearts are hidden from understanding.
Betraying friends for advantage brings failure even to the next generation.
Job has become a proverb and public shame.
Grief dims his sight and turns his body shadow-like.
The upright should be appalled by this.
Still, the righteous hold their way, and clean hands grow stronger.
Job finds no wisdom among the friends.
His days and heart-plans feel broken off.
If Sheol is his house, darkness is his bed.
He imagines the grave as his remaining kin.
So where is hope, and who can see it?
Will hope itself descend with him into dust?
